title: hibbertia lasiopus
intro: Hibbertia lasiopus, also known as Guinea flower, is a small, shrubby plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to have various medicinal properties, including wound healing and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia lasiopus include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Hibbertia lasiopus is often prepared as a tincture or infused oil, and it can also be used as a topical cream or ointment to apply directly to the skin.
